title: Module 1
content:
Control systems:  Basic components of a Control System, Open-Loop
Control Systems and Closed-Loop Control Systems, Examples of control
system, Transfer functions, Block diagram reduction, Signal flow graph and
Mason’s gain formula.
Differential equations for mass, spring and dashpot elements, Mathematical
modelling of control systems - Electrical Systems, Translational and
Rotational Mechanical systems, Analogous systems-force voltage & force-
current analogy - toque-voltage & torque-current analogy.

title: Module 2
content:
Time domain analysis: Continuous systems -standard test signals - step,
ramp, parabolic, impulse - transient and steady state response –first order
systems – second order systems, step responses of first order systems and
second order systems - under damped and over damped systems - time
domain specifications - steady state error – static position, velocity &
acceleration error constants.

title: Module 3
content:
Stability of control systems and Frequency Domain Analysis:
Concept of stability - stability & location of the poles in S-plane - Routh-
Hurwitz stability criterion-Root Locus Method, Construction of root locus-
Effect of poles and zeros and their location on the root locus.
Frequency Response representation- Polar Plot- Relative stability: gain
margin and phase margin, Stability analysis with Bode plot.

title: Module 4
content:
Compensation techniques and Case studies in Mechatronics:
Need for Cascade compensation, Cascade Compensation- PI, PD and PID
controllers – tuning of PID Controller- Lead, Lag and Lead- Lag
compensation- Role of control system in mechatronics-case studies
Automatic temperature control, automatic traffic light control-Automatic
street light control
